title: The Macro Minute: Is it time to book gains in Gold?

Happy Thursday out there 2042, it's your
skipper here at Darius Dale and percent
of macro minute for Thursday, October
16th, 2025. As always, we start with the
executive summary from today's lead off
morning note. If you would like to
review the analysis supporting these
conclusions and to see if you need to
make any changes in your portfolio to
remain on the right side of market risk
or to get on the right side of market
risk to the extent you haven't been
following KISS or Dr. Mo, you need to be
a client of 42 macro.
So, today's key macro question is is it
time to book gains in gold?
Our answer, US Treasury Secretary Scott
Bessent, former client, floated the
possibility of extending the pause on
tariffs for Chinese imports beyond three
months if Beijing abandons its planned
rare earth export controls with the
current 90-day truce set to expire in
November.
Recall that China recently unveiled
rules mandating foreign companies to
secure government authorization before
shipping products containing even
minimal levels of China origin rare
earths. We share consensus view of these
maneuvers as strategic posturing before
a potential meeting of Presidents Trump
and Xi at the Asia-Pacific Economic
Cooperation Summit in South Korea later
this month.
You know who is absolutely loving
Trump's trade war? You. Because you have
more gold in your portfolio than the
average central bank thanks to KISS.
Gold is surging like Bitcoin used to
prior to Wall Street's upside
capitulation into the asset class that
we correctly forecast a half decade ago.
The price of bullion accelerated past
$4,200 an ounce this week with a gain
exceeding 6%.
It's truly humbling to know that we were
among the first in the world to help our
global investor community profit from
the gold is better than sovereign debt
for proper portfolio construction theme.
As someone who grew up in the
drug-infested slums of America
fantasizing over the gold chains adorned
by my favorite 90s hip hop artists, I
never in a million years would have
helped thought I would have helped
author a theme that led to people,
funds, and institutions all over the
world panic buying gold.
Taking a step back, however, I fear what
it means for the outlook for Western
societies, particularly the US and the
US in particular, if the gold rally
doesn't materially correct soon.
It's not like gold is an inconsequential
asset class in the context of human
history.
Asked differently, what does a doubling
plus of the price of gold in 25 months
in the 25 months since we published our
inaugural investing during a
fourth-turning regime presentation say
about the eventuality of paradigm D, the
print phase of the cut-grow-print
sequence required to address the
geopolitically driven supply-demand
imbalance in the Treasury bond market
that we identified in our empirically
based fourth-turning framework?
Whether this fourth turning ends good or
bad for America is not something I'm
smart enough to opine on.
What we are smart enough to do here at
42 macro is help you make and save tons
of money throughout this process and in
the first turn first turning that will
follow it, Lord willing.
Transition to our 42 macro dashboard. As
always, we wrap up with a question from
our community. This one's titled, "Is it
time to sell gold?"
The message in today's lead off morning
note, I assume was no because if it had
been yes, it would have been it would
have shown up in KISS or Dr. Mo. That is
correct. The market noise right now is
that Sorry, the market noise right now
is like the noise from Seattle's Hawk's
Nest, which I'm a big fan of.
It's a relentless narrative machine.
Uh fiscal pundits or financial pundits
ex post an endless tide of headlines
designed to unsettle, distract, and
influence decision-making just like
rattled quarterbacks who show up to
Seattle with the exception of Baker
Mayfield. Shout out to my man, uh Josh
Weiser, offensive coordinator or it's a
coordinator for the Tampa Bay Buccaneers
and one of my best friends, uh soon to
be head coach of the hopefully New York
Giants. We'll take Miami.
It undermines a disciplined process.
This is why I joined 42 macro
to have a North Star that doesn't
respond to the Beast Quake. Uh so,
phenomenal.
Once, beautifully written. Obviously, it
touched my heart as a huge Seahawks fan,
but uh just taking a step back, you
know, I was you know, being interviewed
by a reporter earlier today and you
know, one of the first things he asked
me is like, you know, should you be
responding to the government shutdown or
this or that? And I was like, hey,
most important thing you can do as an
investor is to learn how to separate
signal from noise.
The amount of noise that is being
produced, particularly in this Trump
presidency with this flood the zone
strategy across economic, you know,
geopolitical, and and and social policy
is something that is expressly designed
to do exactly what he wants you to do,
which is overreact and freak out in a
thousand different directions. But there
are only six things that matter to asset
markets on a trend basis. It's growth,
inflation, monetary policy, fiscal
policy, liquidity, and positioning. Four
of those five cycles are moving in an
explicitly positive direction when you
look out over a one-year fourth-turn
horizon.
One of those cycles is moving in the
wrong direction, but it will be
nullified in our opinion by the
structural regime change we're going to
see in the other cycle, which is
monetary policy, overcoming the
inflation cycle. And then finally, the
positioning cycle is not in a great
place, but guess what?
If the if if if the other five cycles,
not only on a net basis, but on an
absolute basis are incredibly robustly
positive, then the positioning cycle
doesn't matter. It's not a catalyst.
Valuation is not a catalyst. Credit
spreads are not a catalyst. You know,
the AI bulls-bears survey is not a
catalyst. The gross exposure market
neutral hedge funds is not a catalyst.
The, you know, the the asset allocation
of investment advisors is not a
catalyst. What's a catalyst is when
something goes wrong in one of those
cycles or multiple of those cycles
enough to freak people out and force
them to change their position. And right
now, in our opinion, we do not see
enough stuff going wrong in those other
five cycles on a durable basis to cause
a market meltdown. Now, it will
eventually come in our opinion. Um it
will certainly come. Paradigm C is not
going to end well, we know that. There
will be a period of time between
paradigm C and paradigm B that will suck
for most investors. Could suck for
years, by the way. You know, we've seen
plenty of financial crises in the
history.
Um
but the the point I'm trying to make
here is that, you know, it when it comes
to, you know, this this narrative
machine, particularly around gold now
because it's really starting to pop up.
You know, I I I laughed out loud earlier
this week with that Bloomberg article
being Wall Street's new trade. I'm like,
oh my god, new?
Uh
the narrative machine around gold is
really, really fervent. I mean, you
know, you go to Costco, people in line
at Costco waiting on those hot dogs are
also buying gold. So, I'm telling you
right now, it's not early in the trade,
but in our view, it's not late in the
trade either when you take a multi-year
time horizon perspective if we're right
on this paradigm B-C-D sequence because
ultimately, we're going to wind up in a
place where
$4,200 an ounce for gold is going to be
very, very, very cheap. So, in our
opinion, hopefully it doesn't mean
something that is incredibly bad for
America. As an American citizen, you
know, I obviously pray that this ends
well for everyone, but
hopefully my prayers were heard
answered, but they may not be, so it's
not my job to uh
enact those outcomes, those policy
outcomes, at least not yet, but it is my
job to help keep our clients on the

doing that with a phenomenal job with
our gold our Dr. Mo signal here on gold,
which has told our clients to be long
gold since October of 2023. So, thank

tomorrow. Have a great day out there.
